meta.book: Lay, Linear Algebra and Its Applications

items.label: 1a
items.text: Every matrix is row equivalent to a unique matrix in echelon form.

items.label: 1b
items.text: Any system of n linear equations in n variables has at most n solutions.

items.label: 1c
items.text: If a system of linear equations has two different solutions, it must have infinitely many solutions.

items.label: 1d
items.text: If a system of linear equations has no free variables, then it has a unique solution.

items.label: 1e
items.text: If an augmented matrix [A  b] is transformed into [C  d] by elementary row operations, then the equations Ax = b and Cx = d have exactly the same solution sets.

items.label: 1f
items.text: If a system Ax = b has more than one solution, then so does the system Ax = 0.

items.label: 1g
items.text: If A is an m×n matrix and the equation Ax = b is consistent for some b, then the columns of A span R^m.

items.label: 1h
items.text: If an augmented matrix [A  b] can be transformed by elementary row operations into reduced echelon form, then the equation Ax = b is consistent.

items.label: 1i
items.text: If matrices A and B are row equivalent, they have the same reduced echelon form.

items.label: 1j
items.text: The equation Ax = 0 has the trivial solution if and only if there are no free variables.

items.label: 1k
items.text: If A is an m×n matrix and the equation Ax = b is consistent for every b in R^m, then A has m pivot columns.

items.label: 1l
items.text: If an m×n matrix A has a pivot position in every row, then the equation Ax = b has a unique solution for each b in R^m.

items.label: 1m
items.text: If an n×n matrix A has n pivot positions, then the reduced echelon form of A is the n×n identity matrix.

items.label: 1n
items.text: If 3×3 matrices A and B each have three pivot positions, then A can be transformed into B by elementary row operations.

items.label: 1o
items.text: If A is an m×n matrix, if the equation Ax = b has at least two different solutions, and if the equation Ax = c is consistent, then the equation Ax = c has many solutions.

items.label: 1p
items.text: If A and B are row equivalent m×n matrices and if the columns of A span R^m, then so do the columns of B.

items.label: 1q
items.text: If none of the vectors in the set S = {v1, v2, v3} in R^3 is a multiple of one of the other vectors, then S is linearly independent.

items.label: 1r
items.text: If {u, v, w} is linearly independent, then u, v, and w are not in R^2.

items.label: 1s
items.text: In some cases, it is possible for four vectors to span R^5.

items.label: 1t
items.text: If u and v are in R^m, then −u is in Span{u, v}.

items.label: 1u
items.text: If u, v, and w are nonzero vectors in R^2, then w is a linear combination of u and v.

items.label: 1v
items.text: If w is a linear combination of u and v in R^n, then u is a linear combination of v and w.

items.label: 1w
items.text: Suppose that v1, v2, and v3 are in R^5, v2 is not a multiple of v1, and v3 is not a linear combination of v1 and v2. Then {v1, v2, v3} is linearly independent.

items.label: 1x
items.text: A linear transformation is a function.

items.label: 1y
items.text: If A is a 6×5 matrix, the linear transformation x ↦ Ax cannot map R^5 onto R^6.

items.label: 1z
items.text: If A is an m×n matrix with m pivot columns, then the linear transformation x ↦ Ax is a one-to-one mapping.
